examples.title: Algorithm: Minimum Distance Classifier
examples.description: This pseudocode demonstrates the minimum distance classifier algorithm.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MinimumDistanceClassifier
INPUT: X_train, y_train, input_image
OUTPUT: class_label

BEGIN
  // Load the dataset
  prototypes = X_train
  class_labels = y_train

  // Calculate the distance between the input image and each prototype
  distances = []
  FOR EACH prototype IN prototypes
    distance = EuclideanDistance(input_image, prototype)
    distances.append(distance)
  END FOR

  // Find the index of the minimum distance
  min_distance_index = ArgMin(distances)

  // Assign the class label based on the minimum distance
  class_label = class_labels[min_distance_index]

  RETURN class_label
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the minimum distance classifier algorithm. It calculates the distance between the input image and each prototype, finds the index of the minimum distance, and assigns the class label based on the minimum distance.

examples.title: Python: Minimum Distance Classifier
examples.description: This Python implementation demonstrates the minimum distance classifier algorithm using Scikit-Learn library.
examples.code:
from sklearn.neighbors import NearestNeighbors
import numpy as np

# Load the dataset
X_train = np.random.rand(100, 10)  # input features
y_train = np.random.randint(0, 3, 100)  # class labels

# Create a minimum distance classifier
nn = NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=1, algorithm='brute', metric='euclidean')
nn.fit(X_train)

# Preprocess the input image
input_image = np.random.rand(10)

# Calculate the distance between the input image and the prototypes
 distances, indices = nn.kneighbors(input_image.reshape(1, -1))

# Assign a class label based on the minimum distance
class_label = y_train[indices[0][0]]
print(class_label)
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the minimum distance classifier algorithm using Scikit-Learn library. It creates a minimum distance classifier, calculates the distance between the input image and the prototypes, and assigns a class label based on the minimum distance.
